learning_outcomes.description: Show a familiarity with major themes, case studies and concepts in contemporary archaeological research at an introductory level.
Demonstrate a basic and general understanding of the practical methods and techniques of archaeological investigation.
Describe and demonstrate basic skills in archaeological analysis and interpretation.
Successfully demonstrate problem solving skills in archaeology
Successfully demonstrate critical analysis and writing skills.

assessments.weighting: <dd>30% Each quiz is worth 5%. Your top 6 quiz scores will be used to calculate your final mark. </dd>
assessments.due_date:
Week 5 - Week 13

Quizzes are completed in the first 20 minutes of the scheduled practical class in Weeks 5–10, 12 and 13 of semester. There is no quiz in Week 11.
assessments.task_description:
• This assessment will test your understanding of the weekly lectures and required readings relating to theory, method and case studies in contemporary archaeological practice.
  • 8 quizzes will be held over the course of the semester in Weeks 5–10, 12 and 13.
  • Each quiz is worth 5%. Your 30% mark will be calculated using the top 6 scores from the 8 weekly quizzes.
  • Each quiz will test content from the preceding week/s lecture and required reading/s. Details will be provided on Blackboard about which lecture and readings will be tested in each quiz.
  • Questions will be in the format of multiple choice (single and multi-answer), true or false, mix and match, short answer, and other multiform formats.
  • Quizzes will be completed in-person during class time using pencils/pens using paper. You must bring your own pens/pencils to class.
  • You will have 20 minutes to complete each quiz.


Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This assessment task will be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will complete and submit the quiz in class during the specified time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment requires you to prepare a formal site recording package for a mock archaeological site modelled on the standard used by Australian cultural heritage practitioners. The site record will include:

  1. A completed site card, recording the location, environmental context, site description, condition, material components, etc. of a mock archaeological site as well as relevant photographs and artefact records.
  2. GIS maps showing site location, site extent, and other required spatial outputs.
  3. A written statement addressing the site's significance and recommendations regarding further work or management actions.

The Site Card will be completed and submitted using the Inspera Assessment platform via a link in the Assessments folder in Blackboard. Guidelines on how to complete the Site Card as well as relevant background information will be provided.

  • Find more information about using Inspera Assessment from the Library website, which provides the latest information for students about using Inspera.
  • To see what the interface looks like, please try out the Inspera demonstration test here. This shows you the assessment interface using some test questions. Remember, Inspera is a university wide-platform, so there may be some question types in the demo that are not relevant to your discipline.
  • To complete the assessment, you will require access to laptop/desktop computer that meets the device requirements for Inspera assessments. It is important that you check that your laptop meets the device requirements for using the Inspera Assessment platform. If you do not own a suitable laptop, you can borrow one from the Library or book use of a desktop computer in the library.


Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI may provide limited support and guidance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy. Students may be required to complete an AI usage declaration and submit it with their assessment. Additional resources are available through UQ AI Student Hub.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This assessment item must be submitted via Inspera on Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
13/10/2026 - 14/10/2026

The examination will be held during your scheduled practical class time in Week 11.
assessments.task_description:
• This closed-book practical examination will test your comprehension of the archaeological methods and subject matter covered in the practical classes from Weeks 5–10. There will be an emphasis on practical knowledge, but will include complementary content from lectures and readings.
  • Questions will be in a range of formats. You will be required to engage with visual and physical stimuli or perform tasks in order to answer some questions.
  • The exam will be completed during your regularly scheduled practical class using pencils/pens using paper. You must bring your own pens/pencils to class.
  • You will have 90 minutes to complete the exam.


Marking criteria: Criteria including rubrics are available in the ‘Assessments’ folder on the course Blackboard site.

AI Use: This assessment task will be completed in-person. The use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be submitted in person to the Course Coordinator/Tutor at the end of the exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
